settings.feed.diversity.title: Diversity
settings.feed.diversity.enabledDesc: Mix content from different authors and topics to avoid echo chambers
settings.feed.diversity.sameAuthorPenalt: Reduce score for posts from authors you've seen recently (0.5 = strong penalty, 1.0 = no penalty)
settings.feed.diversity.sameTopicPenalty: Reduce score for posts with topics you've seen recently (0.5 = strong penalty, 1.0 = no penalty)
settings.feed.diversity.maxConsecutiveSa: Maximum number of posts from the same author in a row (1-10)

settings.feed.recency.halfLifeHoursDesc: Hours after which a post loses 50% of its recency score (6-72 hours)
settings.feed.recency.maxAgeHoursDesc: Maximum age in hours before a post is excluded from feed (24-336 hours)
settings.feed.quality.title: Quality

settings.feed.resetToDefaultsMessage: Reset all feed settings to their default values?
settings.feed.help.diversity.title: Diversity Settings
settings.feed.help.diversity.message: Diversity settings help prevent echo chambers by mixing content from different authors and topics. Lower penalty values mean more diversity in your feed.
settings.feed.help.recency.title: Recency Settings
settings.feed.help.recency.message: Recency settings control how quickly posts lose relevance over time. Half-life is when a post loses 50% of its recency score. Max age is when posts are excluded from your feed.
settings.feed.help.quality.title: Quality Settings
settings.feed.help.quality.message: Quality settings prioritize posts with high engagement rates, meaning posts that get more likes, comments, and boosts relative to their views.

settings.data.resetPersonalizationDesc: Clear all your feed personalization data and start fresh
settings.data.resetPersonalizationMessag:
This will clear all your feed preferences, including:

• Author preferences
• Topic interests
• Post type preferences
• Active hours and language preferences

Your feed will start learning your preferences again from scratch. This action cannot be undone.

fediverse.sheet.step1.title: What is the fediverse?
fediverse.sheet.step1.body: The fediverse is a network of independent, interconnected servers — much like email. Just as a Gmail user can email someone on Outlook, apps like Mastodon and Mention can talk to each other, so people on other servers can follow you and reply to your posts.
fediverse.sheet.step2.title: How sharing works
fediverse.sheet.step2.body: When sharing is on, your public profile and posts can be seen and followed from other servers across the fediverse. Every server sets its own rules, and Mention can't control or moderate how your content appears once it leaves Mention.
fediverse.sheet.step3.title: You're always in control
fediverse.sheet.step3.body: You can turn fediverse sharing off anytime in Settings. When you do, we ask other servers to delete copies of your content — but because those servers are independent, we can't guarantee every copy is removed.
fediverse.settings.title: Fediverse sharing
fediverse.settings.description: Share your profile and posts across the fediverse
fediverse.settings.disableConfirm.title: Turn off fediverse sharing?
fediverse.settings.disableConfirm.messag: People on other servers will no longer see or follow your profile, and your fediverse followers will be removed. We'll ask other servers to delete your content, but this can't be guaranteed.
fediverse.remoteBadge.a11yLabel: This account is from another network

minQuality.description: Keep only posts whose classified quality is at or above the threshold. Posts without a trusted score pass through.

linkCount.label: Link count
linkCount.description: Keep only posts whose number of links is within the range.

noBots.label: No bots
noBots.description: Drop RSS/bridge mirrors and link-only news bots.

tuning.entryDescription: Tune the quality, engagement, and content filters on your For You feed
tuning.intro: These filters shape only your For You discovery feed. Accounts you follow are never filtered.

tuning.signInRequiredDesc: Adjust which quality, engagement, and content filters shape your discovery feed.

minLength.label: Minimum length
minLength.description: Hide very short discovery posts below a character count.

lowEffortGate.label: No low-effort posts
lowEffortGate.description: Hide emoji-only, shortcode-only, and text-empty discovery posts.

nativeEngagement.label: Require engagement or interest
nativeEngagement.description: Hide discovery posts with no real engagement that don't match your interests.

minQuality.label: Minimum quality
minQuality.description: Hide discovery posts whose classified quality is below your threshold. Off by default.
